component.name: EShot
component.summary: Screenshot, annotation, OCR, upload, GIF and video capture tool

description.p: EShot is a native desktop screenshot workflow tool for Windows and Linux. It captures regions, annotates images, copies or saves output, uploads screenshots, extracts text with OCR, pins references, and records GIF or MP4 captures.
On Linux, EShot integrates with KDE Plasma 6 and GNOME Wayland through desktop portals for screenshots, global shortcuts, notifications, and PipeWire screen recording.
Adds a dedicated animated window capture mode on Windows, improves recording defaults and controls, and fixes Windows tray startup, audio device, and packaged SVG icon issues.
Fixes false missing AAC encoder errors on Linux when GStreamer plugin discovery takes longer during its first run.

Improves draggable capture-safe recording controls on Windows and Linux, fixes KDE Plasma full-screen recording geometry, adds experimental GNOME Wayland integration, and expands Linux portal and package-manager compatibility.

Adds automatic OCR language selection, a configurable capture help panel, dynamic settings tab sizing, immediate update state sharing, safer KDE Wayland capture authorization, and allows unrelated settings to save when an unchanged hotkey is unavailable.
Redesigns the GIF and video recording controls, adds GIF pause and resume, fixes clickable KDE Plasma notifications, and prevents translation tests from changing the saved application language.
Adds DNF dependency setup for Fedora and Nobara, localizes setup errors, makes the tray respond to a normal click, and falls back to the desktop shortcut portal when KDE cannot claim PrintScreen.
Rebuilds the Linux AppImage for baseline x86-64 CPUs and older glibc versions instead of inheriting CachyOS build-host requirements.
Reduces KDE Plasma 6 capture latency by preparing direct KWin screenshot access for integrated AppImages while preserving the cursor-free Spectacle fallback.
Polishes capture annotations, improves KDE Plasma 6 integration, fixes cursor-free Wayland screenshots and AppImage autostart, and restores reliable text entry on KDE/XWayland.
Adds the complete Linux and KDE Plasma 6 port, AppImage and native packages, Wayland capture and recording, Linux desktop integration, and selectable Google Lens or Yandex visual search.
Windows maintenance release preceding the experimental Linux port.
